questions.question: What action hides the Notion sidebar for a more focused workspace?

options.explanation: Clicking the left‑pointing arrows collapses the sidebar.
The space bar does not control the sidebar visibility.
Double‑clicking a page opens it but does not hide the sidebar.

options.text: Select "Hide Sidebar" from the Settings menu
options.explanation: There is no dedicated "Hide Sidebar" option in Settings.

questions.question: Which section of the sidebar would you use to quickly access pages you have marked as favorites?

options.explanation: Teamspaces group pages by team, not by favorite status.
Shared shows pages shared with or by you, not specifically favorites.
The Favorites section is designed for quick access to pages you have favorited.
Private is for personal, unpublished pages, not a favorites list.

questions.question: If you need to locate a page but you only remember part of its title, which sidebar feature should you use?

options.explanation: Inbox shows notifications, not a search function.
The Search feature in the sidebar lets you find pages by name or content.
Trash contains deleted items; it does not search active pages.
The New page button creates a fresh page, it does not search existing ones.

questions.question: What recent change to the sidebar’s notification area is highlighted in the guide?

options.explanation: The guide states that the former "Updates" menu is now called "Inbox" with a refreshed UI.
The change was the opposite direction; Updates became Inbox.
No "Alerts" section is mentioned in the article.

options.text: Notifications are now only available via email
options.explanation: Notifications still appear in the sidebar; they haven’t moved to email only.

questions.question: Which of the following actions cannot be performed directly from the sidebar?

options.explanation: The guide says you can create Teamspaces from the sidebar.
You can freely reorder Teamspaces in the sidebar.
Renaming a workspace is done in Settings, not directly from the sidebar.
Clicking the workspace name in the sidebar lets you switch workspaces.

questions.question: How does the guide describe the purpose of the Private section in the sidebar?

options.text: A place for pages that are shared with external collaborators
options.explanation: Shared pages belong to the Shared section, not Private.

options.text: A secret hideout for brainstorming, planning, or just thinking
options.explanation: The guide explicitly calls Private a "secret hideout" for personal work.

options.text: A repository for archived Teamspace content
options.explanation: Archived content isn’t stored in Private; the Trash handles deletions.

options.text: The default location for all newly created pages
options.explanation: New pages can be placed anywhere; there’s no automatic default to Private.

questions.question: Which combination of steps will let you permanently remove a page from Notion?

options.text: Delete the page, then empty the Trash section
options.explanation: Pages are first moved to Trash; emptying Trash permanently deletes them.

options.text: Move the page to the Private section and then delete it
options.explanation: Moving to Private does not delete; you still need to use Trash.

options.text: Remove the page from Favorites and then close Notion
options.explanation: Removing from Favorites only unmarks it; it does not delete the page.

options.text: Archive the page from the Shared section
options.explanation: Archiving is not mentioned; only Trash handles deletion.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of customizing Settings and Members from the sidebar according to the guide?

options.text: It allows you to change the color scheme of each page individually
options.explanation: Page‑level color changes are not part of Settings and Members.

options.text: It lets you adjust workspace settings and personalize your Notion experience
options.explanation: The guide states Settings and Members let you adjust workspace settings and customize the experience.

options.text: It automatically syncs your Notion data with external cloud services
options.explanation: Syncing with external clouds isn’t mentioned as a Settings function.

options.text: It creates a backup copy of every page in your Private section
options.explanation: Backups are not described as a feature of Settings and Members.
